docs.text: Secluso User Docs ¶ This guide walks you through setting up Secluso in three steps: Getting the app Adding a relay Pairing with your camera Before you begin Have these ready: An Android or iOS phone Your Secluso camera, powered on Your home Wi-Fi network name and password A Secluso relay (hosted account or your own server) Get started
docs.title: Home

docs.text: This guide walks you through setting up Secluso in three steps: Getting the app Adding a relay Pairing with your camera Before you begin Have these ready: An Android or iOS phone Your Secluso camera, powered on Your home Wi-Fi network name and password A Secluso relay (hosted account or your own server) Get started
docs.title: Secluso User Docs

docs.text: Getting the Mobile App ¶ Install the Secluso app from your phone's app store. Android (Google Play) iOS (App Store) Open the Google Play Store (Android) or App Store (iOS). Search for Secluso . Tap Install / Get , then open the app. Look for the right app The publisher is Secluso on Google Play and John Kaczman on the App Store. Make sure it matches before installing. Permissions you'll be asked for later During setup the app requests a few permissions, each used locally: Permission Why it's needed Nearby devices / Wi-Fi To find and connect directly to your camera Notifications To alert you about motion, people, and system events App installed Once the app opens to the "Let's get you set up" screen, you're ready for the next step. Next: Adding a relay
docs.title: Getting the app

Next: Adding a relay
docs.title: Getting the Mobile App

docs.text: Pairing with Your Camera ¶ This is the final step. Your phone forms a direct, encrypted connection to the camera, hands it your Wi-Fi details, and exchanges keys. The app guides you through pairing in three numbered steps. From the System tab or Home screen, tap Add your first camera to begin. Step 1: Connect to the camera ¶ The app discovers your camera over a temporary, direct link. You'll see two quick system prompts. Approve both. 1. Tap Allow so the app can find nearby devices. 2. The app searches for your camera. 3. Tap Connect to join the camera's temporary Secluso Wi-Fi. Why a temporary Wi-Fi network? The camera briefly broadcasts its own Secluso network so your phone can reach it directly, before it's on your home Wi-Fi. This is how the first encrypted handshake happens. Step 2: Enter camera details ¶ Tell the camera who it is and how to reach your home network, then tap Continue . Field What to enter Camera Name A friendly label, e.g. Front Door Wi-Fi Network Your home network name (SSID) Wi-Fi Password Your home network password Use your everyday Wi-Fi details Enter the network the camera should join permanently : your normal home Wi-Fi, not the temporary Secluso network from Step 1. Your Wi-Fi password is never stored Credentials are sent directly to the camera over the encrypted link. They are never saved on your phone or any server. Step 3: Pair and exchange keys ¶ The app sets up encryption with the camera, progressing through three stages. Approve notifications so you can receive alerts. Encrypted channel, then key exchange, then camera config. Tap Allow to get motion and person alerts. Done: camera paired ¶ When pairing finishes, your camera is Connected with E2EE Active . Tap Go to Home , or Add Another Camera to repeat the process. Using your camera ¶ The Home tab lists your cameras and recent activity. Tap a camera to view live, see activity, and find saved clips. Open the Settings tab to choose your push delivery method and toggle Person , Motion , and System alerts independently. Not receiving alerts? Make sure Push Notifications is on in Settings, that you tapped Allow on the notification prompt during pairing, and that a push delivery method is selected.
docs.title: Pairing with your camera

docs.title: Using your camera

docs.text: Adding a Relay ¶ The relay forwards traffic between your cameras and your phone, so you can watch your camera from anywhere. It only ever handles encrypted blobs it cannot read. Step 1: Start the connection ¶ On the Home screen, tap Scan QR code under Connect your relay . Step 2: Choose your relay ¶ Open the System tab and pick how to run the relay: Secluso Relay : scan the QR code from your Secluso account. Self-Hosted : run the relay on your own VPS or dedicated host. Credentials stay on your device Whichever option you choose, your credentials and encryption keys stay on your phone. Secluso cannot access your cameras. Step 3: Confirm it's connected ¶ After scanning, the System tab shows Relay Connected , with your phone linked and the endpoint, protocol, and last sync time listed. Relay ready With a green Relay Connected status, you can add a camera next. Use Check for Updates or Remove Relay from this screen at any time. Next: Pairing with your camera
docs.title: Adding a relay
